trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Canada exports canola, wheat, copper, and aircraft to Turkey. Turkey exports vehicles, textiles, chemicals, and processed foods to Canada. Turkey is an important emerging market for Canadian exports.
disputes: Canada suspended drone component exports to Turkey after Azerbaijan-Armenia conflict (2020)

diplomatic.summary: NATO allies with a complex relationship. Canada has been critical of Turkey's democratic backsliding, its purchase of Russian S-400 systems, and its role in the Azerbaijan-Armenia conflict. Turkey and Canada have periodic friction over Kurdish issues and arms exports. Both remain aligned in NATO framework.
recent_incidents: Canada suspended drone exports to Turkey after use in Nagorno-Karabakh (2020)
Turkey NATO posture complicates Canada-Turkey defense ties

military.summary: Both are NATO allies but with significant friction. Canada supplies components used in Turkish Bayraktar drones. Canada suspended exports after the Nagorno-Karabakh conflict. Turkey's S-400 purchase from Russia remains a point of tension.
incidents: Canada suspended drone component exports to Turkey (2020-2022)

financial.summary: Limited bilateral investment. Some Canadian mining companies have Turkish operations. Turkish construction companies have some Canadian market presence.

energy.summary: Turkey is interested in Canadian LNG as part of energy diversification. Limited current energy trade. Canada exports some agricultural feedstocks used in Turkish energy sector.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific cooperation. Some Turkish students study in Canada. Joint research in engineering, computer science, and materials science.
